title: String growth arithmetic can wrap before reserve

direct_impact.operation: size_t addition wraps before reserve and memcpy() writes before the destination buffer
direct_impact.bytes: validated write of 8 bytes before the target buffer after wraparound

exploitability.reliability: high once the wrapped-size state is reached

chain_potential.standalone: memory corruption followed by deterministic process abort in consumers that compile the vulnerable upb tokenizer/text helpers from source
chain_potential.with_other_bugs: could strengthen a larger exploit chain as a write-primitive precursor, but the required near-SIZE_MAX state makes practical exploitation difficult

justification.AV: L - The validated path is not present in the shipped libupb.a archive; exploitation requires a local/downstream build that embeds the vulnerable upb tokenizer sources.
justification.AC: H - Triggering the corruption requires driving string state near SIZE_MAX before a final append, which is an exceptional precondition.
justification.PR: N - No privileges are required beyond supplying the hostile input/state to the vulnerable parser helper.
justification.UI: N - The write occurs during ordinary parser/helper execution.
justification.S: U - Impact remains within the vulnerable process.
justification.C: N - No information disclosure was demonstrated.
justification.I: L - The memcpy() destination underflows the buffer boundary and corrupts adjacent memory, but controlled post-corruption effects were not shown.
justification.A: H - The confirmed result is a reliable ASan/UBSan-detected abort once the wrapped state is hit.

notes: Reachability is materially limited because the provided libupb.a does not contain tokenizer/string objects; the issue affects downstream source consumers rather than the shipped archive in this workspace.
Severity is capped at MEDIUM because the validated effect is crash-oriented corruption under an extreme size precondition, not a demonstrated control-flow hijack.
